top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 100, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Gran Fondo Florida's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 100 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Florida's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
Gran Fondo Florida's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Florida. Train it until it feels boring.
At Gran Fondo Florida, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 100 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
100 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Florida, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,248ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.heat: March in Dade City, Florida, USA can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Gran Fondo Florida. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Florida’s 100 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Florida requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 100 miles of mixed terrain in Dade City, Florida, USA.
